---
title: "GreyVsRed"
output: html_document
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(giscoR)
library(sf)
library(broom)

red <- read.csv("records-2026-06-16/records-2026-06-16.csv") |> 
  rename(Longitude = Longitude..WGS84., Latitude = Latitude..WGS84.)
grey <- read.csv("records-2026-06-16-Sciurus-carolinensis/records-2026-06-16-Sciurus-carolinensis.csv") |> 
  rename(Longitude = Longitude..WGS84., Latitude = Latitude..WGS84.)
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
uk <- gisco_get_countries(country="UK")

# 1. Project the UK layer
uk_projected <- st_transform(uk, 27700)

# 2. Get the bounding box area of the UK
bbox <- st_bbox(uk_projected)
bbox_area <- (bbox$xmax - bbox$xmin) * (bbox$ymax - bbox$ymin)

# 3. Calculate the target size for ~100 hexagons
# The area of a regular hexagon is approximately 0.866 * d^2 (where d is cellsize diameter)
target_hex_area <- bbox_area / 1000
hex_cellsize <- sqrt(target_hex_area / 0.866)

# 4. Create the hex grid
hex_grid <- st_make_grid(uk_projected, cellsize = hex_cellsize, square = FALSE) %>%
  st_sf() %>%
  mutate(region_id = row_number())

# 5. Clip it cleanly to the UK coastline
uk_hex_grid <- st_intersection(hex_grid, st_union(uk_projected))

create_density <- function(points, sfc) {
  # 1. Project your points
  points_sf <- st_as_sf(points, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326) %>%
    st_transform(27700)
  
  # 2. Spatial Join
  points_in_hex <- st_join(points_sf, sfc, join = st_intersects)
  
  # 3. Count points per hexagon
  hex_counts <- points_in_hex %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    group_by(region_id) %>%
    summarise(point_count = n())
  
  # 4. Join counts back to the hexagonal map shapes
  sfc %>%
    left_join(hex_counts, by = "region_id") %>%
    mutate(point_count = replace_na(point_count, 0))
}
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
graph_uk_density <- function(year) {
  combined_density <- bind_rows(
    mutate(create_density(red |> filter(Year==year), uk_hex_grid), Squirrel = "Red Squirrels"),
    mutate(create_density(grey |> filter(Year==year), uk_hex_grid), Squirrel = "Grey Squirrels")
  )
  
  ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data=uk_projected, color = "grey100") +
    geom_sf(data=combined_density, aes(fill = point_count, alpha = point_count), colour="white") +
    facet_wrap(~Squirrel) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "viridis", name = "Sightings") +
    scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0, 2.5), guide = "none") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = sprintf("Sightings of Red vs Grey Squirrels in %d", year),
         caption = gisco_attributions()) +
    theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(), 
          axis.title.x=element_blank(),
          axis.text.y=element_blank(), 
          axis.title.y=element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank())
}

graph_uk_density(2020)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
sightings <- bind_rows(
    mutate(red |> group_by(Year) |> summarise(n = n()) |> arrange(desc(n)), Squirrel = "Red"),
    mutate(grey |> group_by(Year) |> summarise(n = n()) |> arrange(desc(n)), Squirrel = "Grey")
  ) |> 
  pivot_wider(names_from = Squirrel, values_from = c(n), values_fill = 0) |>
  mutate(Total = Red + Grey, Prop = Red / Total) |>
  filter(Total > 50)

fit <- glm(Prop ~ Year, weight=Total, data=sightings, family=binomial)

ggplot(sightings, aes(x = Year, y = Prop)) +
  geom_smooth(method="glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"), 
              aes(weight = Total), se = TRUE) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(), limits = c(0, NA)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "The Decline of the Red Squirrel Share in the UK",
    subtitle = "Proportion of total annual squirrel sightings that were Red\nCurve fitted using a binomial GLM (weighted by annual sample size)",
    y = "Share of Total Sightings",
    x = "Year"
  )
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
nuts <- gisco_get_nuts(country="UK", nuts_level = "3", year=2016) %>%
  st_transform(27700) %>%
  mutate(region_id = row_number())

yearA <- 1990
yearB <- 2026

nuts_density <- bind_rows(
  mutate(red %>% filter(Year >= yearA) %>% group_by(Year) %>% group_modify(~ create_density(.x, nuts)), Squirrel="Red"),
  mutate(grey %>% filter(Year >= yearA) %>% group_by(Year) %>% group_modify(~ create_density(.x, nuts)), Squirrel="Grey"),
) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Squirrel, values_from = point_count) %>%
  mutate(Total = Red + Grey, Prop = Red / Total) %>% 
  filter(Total > 50)

nuts_density_models <- nuts_density %>%
  group_by(NUTS_NAME) %>%
  filter(sum(Red > 0) >= 3) %>% # Eliminate extinction possibility
  summarise(fit = list(glm(Prop ~ Year, weight=Total, family=binomial, data=pick(everything()))))

change_in_prop <- nuts_density_models %>% 
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(pA = predict(fit, newdata=data.frame(Year=yearA), type="response"), 
         pB = predict(fit, newdata=data.frame(Year=yearB), type="response"), 
         Change = pB - pA)

library(ggpattern)

ggplot() + 
  geom_sf(data=nuts, colour="gray70") +
  geom_sf(data=left_join(nuts, change_in_prop, by="NUTS_NAME"), aes(fill = Change)) +
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, limits = c(-1, 1), oob = scales::squish, labels = scales::label_percent()) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = sprintf("Red Squirrel Displacement Across the UK (%d-%d)", yearA, yearB),
       subtitle = "Modeled absolute change in the Red Squirrel share of total sightings",
       caption = paste("Negative (blue) values indicate Red displacement by Grey squirrels.\n", gisco_attributions()),
       fill = "Net Shift in\nPopulation Share") +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(), 
        axis.title.x=element_blank(),
        axis.text.y=element_blank(), 
        axis.title.y=element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        plot.caption.position = 'plot',
        plot.title.position = 'plot',
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
extinct <- nuts_density %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(NUTS_ID, NUTS_NAME) %>%
  filter(Year >= 1990 & sum(Red > 0) <= 3) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  distinct(NUTS_ID, NUTS_NAME)

extinct_map_data <- nuts %>%
  left_join(extinct, by = c("NUTS_ID", "NUTS_NAME")) %>%
  mutate(Status = ifelse(NUTS_ID %in% extinct$NUTS_ID, "Functionally Extinct", "Active/Present"))

ggplot(data = extinct_map_data) + 
  geom_sf(aes(fill = Status), color = "white", size = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Functionally Extinct" = "grey80", "Active/Present" = "darkred")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Red Squirrel Functional Extinction Zones (1990-2026)")
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthdata)
library(spatialRF)
library(parallel)
library(tidyterra)
library(geodata)
library(terra)

cluster <- parallel::makeCluster(
  parallel::detectCores() - 1,
  type = "PSOCK"
)

#Marvin N. Wright, Andreas Ziegler (2017). ranger: A Fast Implementation of Random Forests for High Dimensional Data in C++ and R. Journal of Statistical Software, 77(1), 1-17. doi:10.18637/jss.v077.i01
#Blas M. Benito (2025). spatialRF: Easy Spatial Regression with Random Forest. R package version 1.1.5. doi: 10.5281/zenodo.17992636. url: https://blasbenito.github.io/spatialRF/

# Marston, C.; Rowland, C.S.; O'Neil, A.W.; Morton, R.D. (2022). Land Cover Map 2021 (1km summary rasters, GB and N. Ireland). NERC EDS Environmental Information Data Centre. https://doi.org/10.5285/a3ff9411-3a7a-47e1-9b3e-79f21648237d
uk_land_cover <- terra::rast("land_cover_data/gb2021lcm1km_dominant_aggregate.tif")

# Carnell, E.; Tomlinson, S.J.; Reis, S. (2025). UK gridded population at 1 km resolution for 2021 based on Census 2021/2022 and Land Cover Map 2021. NERC EDS Environmental Information Data Centre. https://doi.org/10.5285/7beefde9-c520-4ddf-897a-0167e8918595
uk_population <- terra::rast("population_data/uk_residential_population_2021.tif")

# Jarvis A., H.I. Reuter, A. Nelson, E. Guevara, 2008, Hole-filled seamless SRTM data V4, International Centre for Tropical Agriculture (CIAT), available from https://srtm.csi.cgiar.org.
uk_elevation <- geodata::elevation_30s("gb", path = tempdir())

forest_features <- terra::extract(uk_land_cover, vect(nuts), fun = function(x) {
  total <- length(x)
  pct_coniferous <- sum(x == 2) / total
  pct_broadleaf  <- sum(x == 1) / total
  return(c(pct_conif = pct_coniferous, pct_broad = pct_broadleaf))
})

population_features <- terra::extract(uk_population, vect(nuts), fun = function(x) { return(sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)) })

elevation_features <- terra::extract(uk_elevation, vect(nuts), fun = function(x) { return(mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)) })
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
nuts_density %>% 
  select(Year, Red, region_id) %>% 
  left_join(elevation_features, by = c("region_id" = "ID")) %>%
  left_join(population_features, by = c("region_id" = "ID")) %>%
  left_join(forest_features, by = c("region_id" = "ID"), copy=TRUE)
```
